meta.title: -dom

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Every word and meaning transcribed faithfully; the teaching layer (mentor_note, discovery) is authored for CAT OS.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Word DNA milestone); WORD_DNA_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: The batch's first Germanic-origin suffix, deliberately placed after two Greek/Latin ones so the Tree already feels like more than one language by this point. Owner review pending.

unit.label: -dom
unit.core_meaning: a state, condition, or domain of
unit.mentor_note: The smallest suffixes often carry the oldest ideas. This one has been naming domains and conditions for a thousand years.

members.meaning: a state or territory ruled by a king
the collective state or domain of officials
the territory or rank belonging to a duke

predict_options.text: A state, condition, or domain of
A group of people who dislike something
An old or outdated version of something

discovery.understand_note: Dom names a state or a domain. Freedom is the state of being free. A kingdom is the domain a king rules. Officialdom is the whole condition of being run by officials.

applies.prompt: Knowing -dom names a state or domain, what do you think a dukedom is?

options.text: The territory or rank belonging to a duke
A formal title given only to kings
A group of people who admire a duke
